UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION

Washington, D.C. 20549

FORM 10-K

Annual Report Pursuant to Section 13 or 15(d)

For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024

SAMPLE CORPORATION

Commission file number: 001-12345

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

We are a technology company focused on artificial intelligence infrastructure. Our platform enables enterprises to deploy machine learning models at scale.

We were incorporated in Delaware in 2010. As of December 31, 2024, we had approximately 5,200 full-time employees worldwide.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

The following risk factors could materially adversely affect our business, financial condition, and results of operations.

Competition: The market for AI infrastructure is intensely competitive and rapidly evolving. We compete with companies that have significantly greater financial and technical resources.

Customer Concentration: Our top 10 customers represent approximately 45% of revenue. Loss of any major customer could materially impact our results.

ITEM 2. PROPERTIES

Our principal offices are located at 123 Main Street, San Francisco, CA 94105. We lease approximately 200,000 square feet of office space.

ITEM 3. LEGAL PROCEEDINGS

We are subject to various legal proceedings in the ordinary course of business. We do not believe any pending litigation will have a material adverse effect.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

Overview: Revenue increased 22% to $3.2 billion for fiscal year 2024, compared to $2.6 billion in fiscal year 2023.

Gross profit margin was 68% in 2024, up from 65% in 2023, reflecting operating leverage as we scaled our platform.

Accounts receivable increased 18% to $420 million at December 31, 2024, compared to $356 million at December 31, 2023. Days sales outstanding was 48 days in 2024, consistent with 2023.

Goodwill and intangible assets totaled $890 million at December 31, 2024, representing approximately 22% of total assets of $4.1 billion.

Operating cash flow was $620 million in 2024, up 35% from $459 million in 2023.

ITEM 7A. QUANTITATIVE AND QUALITATIVE DISCLOSURES ABOUT MARKET RISK

We are exposed to market risk in the ordinary course of our business. Market risk represents the risk of loss that may impact our financial position.

ITEM 8. FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND SUPPLEMENTARY DATA

Report of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm

To the Stockholders and Board of Directors of Sample Corporation:

Opinion on the Financial Statements

We have audited the accompanying consolidated balance sheets of Sample Corporation as of December 31, 2024 and 2023, and the related consolidated statements of operations, comprehensive income, stockholders equity, and cash flows for each of the three years in the period ended December 31, 2024.

In our opinion, the financial statements present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of Sample Corporation as of December 31, 2024 and 2023, and the results of its operations and its cash flows for each of the three years in the period ended December 31, 2024, in conformity with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America.

/s/ PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP

San Francisco, California

February 15, 2025

ITEM 9. CHANGES IN AND DISAGREEMENTS WITH ACCOUNTANTS

None.

ITEM 9A. CONTROLS AND PROCEDURES

Disclosure Controls and Procedures: Our principal executive officer and principal financial officer have evaluated the effectiveness of our disclosure controls and procedures as of December 31, 2024. Based on this evaluation, they concluded that our disclosure controls and procedures were effective.

ITEM 15. EXHIBITS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENT SCHEDULES
